channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: On Camera, Man Drives Into Pedestrians, Then Chases People With Knife In Italy

media:content.media:description: The man rammed his car into a sidewalk with pedestrians.

item.description: Four of the persons were critically injured in the incident, Modena mayor Massimo Mezzetti told reporters.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1100241/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Four of the persons were critically injured in the incident, Modena mayor Massimo Mezzetti told reporters.

item.title: Netherlands Returns 1,000-Year-Old Chola-Era Copper Plates To India

media:content.media:description: The handover marked the culmination of years of diplomatic efforts by New Delhi

item.description: The artefacts, widely referred to in Europe as the 'Leiden Plates', are among the most historically valuable surviving records from the Chola empire. India had been seeking their return since 2012.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1100200/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The artefacts, widely referred to in Europe as the 'Leiden Plates', are among the most historically valuable surviving records from the Chola empire. India had been seeking their return since 2012.

item.title: Rescue Diver Dies In Search Of 4 Italians Who Drowned In Maldives

media:content.media:description: The group of five Italian divers is believed to have died.

item.description: Mohamed Mahudhee, a member of the Maldivian National Defense Force, died of underwater decompression sickness.
item.content:encoded: Mohamed Mahudhee, a member of the Maldivian National Defense Force, died of underwater decompression sickness.

item.title: PM Modi Meets Dutch Royals In Netherlands, Discusses Tech, Trade

media:content.media:description: Later on Saturday, PM Modi is scheduled to meet his Dutch counterpart Rob Jetten.

item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday met King of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima at the Royal Palace and discussed close cooperation in digital technology, innovation, fintech and blue economy.
item.content:encoded: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Saturday met King of the Netherlands Willem-Alexander and Queen Maxima at the Royal Palace and discussed close cooperation in digital technology, innovation, fintech and blue economy.

item.title: Bangladesh Arrests Ex-Soldier Who Threatened To Attack Parliament, Airport

media:content.media:description: Counterterrorism officers were questioning Mahed, who police said was linked to a radical Islamist group.

item.description: The suspect, named as 23-year-old Mohammad Rahed Hossain Mahed, "had taken refuge in a hideout near the Indian border", authorities said.
item.content:encoded: The suspect, named as 23-year-old Mohammad Rahed Hossain Mahed, "had taken refuge in a hideout near the Indian border", authorities said.

item.title: Palestinians Forced To Destroy Own Homes For "Disneyfied" Israeli Theme Park

media:content.media:description: A Palestinian man inspects the rubble of his house after it was demolished by Israeli forces

item.description: The forced clearing of the Al-Bustan neighbourhood in the larger Silwan district aims to make way for a state-backed biblical theme park called the King's Garden Park.
item.content:encoded: The forced clearing of the Al-Bustan neighbourhood in the larger Silwan district aims to make way for a state-backed biblical theme park called the King's Garden Park.

item.title: 1996 Military Jet Killings: Why Team Trump Wants Cuba's Raul Castro Charged

media:content.media:description: The Trump administration plans to unveil criminal charges against Cuba's former president, Raul Castro.

item.description: On February 24, 1996, Cuban jets shot down two small planes being flown by Brothers to the Rescue, a group of Cuban exile pilots.
item.content:encoded: On February 24, 1996, Cuban jets shot down two small planes being flown by Brothers to the Rescue, a group of Cuban exile pilots.

item.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Hamas Armed Wing Chief, 'Architect' Of October 7 Attack

media:content.media:description: Ezzedine Al-Haddad was one of the last senior commanders of the Hamas military wing.

item.description: Ezzedine Al-Haddad had been killed in an Israeli strike on a residential apartment and a civilian vehicle in Gaza City on Friday.
item.content:encoded: Ezzedine Al-Haddad had been killed in an Israeli strike on a residential apartment and a civilian vehicle in Gaza City on Friday.

item.title: "They Will Take Out Gun": Russian Foreign Minister Warns Journalist At Delhi Briefing

media:content.media:description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov was addressing a press conference in New Delhi

item.description: The interaction was interrupted multiple times by a journalist who was allegedly speaking on the phone while Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov was answering questions.
item.content:encoded: The interaction was interrupted multiple times by a journalist who was allegedly speaking on the phone while Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov was answering questions.

item.title: "We Spy Like Hell On Them": Trump Responds To Question On China Cyberattacks

media:content.media:description: US and China are locked in a persistent cyber espionage and intelligence-gathering rivalry

item.description: When questioned by reporters on whether he spoke to Chinese President Xi Jinping about the cyberattacks that China has done in the US, President Trump said, "I did. He talked about attacks that we did in China. You know, what they do, we do too."
item.content:encoded: When questioned by reporters on whether he spoke to Chinese President Xi Jinping about the cyberattacks that China has done in the US, President Trump said, "I did. He talked about attacks that we did in China. You know, what they do, we do too."

item.title: 8 Dead, 20 Injured After Cargo Train Rams Bus In Bangkok

media:content.media:description: The impact also dragged several nearby vehicles along the tracks.

item.description: Videos of the crash shared on social media showed a line of vehicles had stopped at a railway crossing when a cargo train struck an orange bus.
item.content:encoded: Videos of the crash shared on social media showed a line of vehicles had stopped at a railway crossing when a cargo train struck an orange bus.

item.title: Diver Mauled To Death By 16-Foot Shark In Australian Tourist Hotspot

media:content.media:description: Australia has averaged more than 3 fatal shark attacks a year in recent decades. (Representational Image)

item.description: The 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with a friend when he was attacked on a coral reef off Rottnest Island near Australia's southwest coast.
item.content:encoded: The 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with a friend when he was attacked on a coral reef off Rottnest Island near Australia's southwest coast.

item.title: PM Modi Netherlands Visit LIVE: "Felt Like Being In India": PM Modi praises Indians In Netherlands
item.description: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a five-nation tour from May 15 to 20, during which he visited the United Arab Emirates as part of the first leg of the visit.
item.content:encoded: PM Modi in Netherlands LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is on a five-nation tour from May 15 to 20, during which he visited the United Arab Emirates as part of the first leg of the visit.

item.title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Israel, Lebanon Extend Ceasfire, Trump Plans More Iran Strikes
item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping today in a high-stakes summit between the two countries.

item.title: Indian Mangoes Make Fresh Entry In Seattle After Year-Long Outreach Efforts

media:content.media:description: The Seattle consulate hosted a mango promotion event last year

item.description: The renewed spotlight on Indian mangoes came after a special segment aired on Fox 13 News' "Good Day Seattle", where India's Consul General in Seattle, Prakash Gupta, spoke about the arrival
item.content:encoded: The renewed spotlight on Indian mangoes came after a special segment aired on Fox 13 News' "Good Day Seattle", where India's Consul General in Seattle, Prakash Gupta, spoke about the arrival

item.title: 2,000-Year-Old Egyptian Mummy Found With Ancient Greek War Poem: Report

media:content.media:description: The mummy belonged to a non-royal man from the Roman period.

item.description: The discovery was made at Oxyrhynchus, an ancient burial site in central Egypt, by a team from the University of Barcelona
item.content:encoded: The discovery was made at Oxyrhynchus, an ancient burial site in central Egypt, by a team from the University of Barcelona

item.title: YouTube, Snap Settle School District's Social Media Addiction Claims

media:content.media:description: The companies say they take extensive steps to keep teens and young users safe on their platforms.

item.description: Alphabet's YouTube and Snap have reached settlements in the first case set for trial in litigation seeking to force social media platforms to
item.content:encoded: Alphabet's YouTube and Snap have reached settlements in the first case set for trial in litigation seeking to force social media platforms to

item.title: Putin To Visit China On May 19-20 After Trump Trip

media:content.media:description: Putin and Xi will "exchange views on key international and regional issues".

item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump's trip to...
item.content:encoded: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to China on May 19 for a two-day visit, hot on the heels of US President Donald Trump's trip to...

item.title: Scientists Find Climate Change Is Reducing Oxygen In Rivers Worldwide

media:content.media:description: Scientists worry that oxygen levels in rivers could fall so low that dead zones appear.

item.description: Warmer water, which happens with human-caused climate change, releases more oxygen into the atmosphere.
item.content:encoded: Warmer water, which happens with human-caused climate change, releases more oxygen into the atmosphere.

item.title: "Thought He Could Hide": Trump Says Top ISIS Leader Abu-Bilal Killed
item.description: US Kills ISIS "Second-In-Command" Globally Abu-Bilal-Al-Minuki In Africa, Says Donald Trump
item.content:encoded: US Kills ISIS "Second-In-Command" Globally Abu-Bilal-Al-Minuki In Africa, Says Donald Trump
